name: Massless up quark (historical strong-CP solution)
summary: Historical proposal that the bare up-quark mass is zero, with the apparent non-zero physical up-mass arising from QCD instanton-induced 't Hooft vertices (the Kaplan-Manohar ambiguity). A vanishing bare m_u eliminates the QCD theta angle as a physical parameter, automatically solving the strong-CP problem without an axion. Lattice QCD has now ruled out this solution.

addresses_puzzles.note: Conceptually elegant — m_u = 0 makes theta unphysical via the chiral rotation. Excluded by lattice QCD: Alexandrou et al. (2020), arXiv:2002.07802, 'Ruling Out the Massless Up-Quark Solution to the Strong CP Problem by Computing the Topological Mass Contribution with Lattice QCD', PRL 125, 232001. Listed for graph completeness.

touches_benchmarks.note: Lattice QCD now firmly establishes m_u != 0 from chiral perturbation theory + non-perturbative mass-renormalization, with the Kaplan-Manohar ambiguity directly computed and shown to be insufficient.

new_symmetries: axial U(1)_A acting on up quark
introduces.note: Conceptually parameter-free: removes one parameter (theta) by setting another (m_u) to zero. But m_u != 0 is now established empirically.

history: Discussed since the 1970s in various forms. Kaplan and Manohar (1986), 'Current-mass ratios of the light quarks', Phys. Rev. Lett. 56, 2004 — pointed out the ambiguity in extracting bare m_u from chiral Lagrangian observables. Definitively excluded by Alexandrou-Athenodorou-Cichy-Constantinou-Hadjiyiannakou-Jansen-Kostrzewa-Petros-Steffens-Stylianou-Urbach (2020), arXiv:2002.07802.

references.citation: Kaplan and Manohar, 'Current-mass ratios of the light quarks', Phys. Rev. Lett. 56, 2004 (1986)
Alexandrou et al., 'Ruling Out the Massless Up-Quark Solution to the Strong CP Problem by Computing the Topological Mass Contribution with Lattice QCD', Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 232001 (2020)
